takeaways: Senior housing demand hits all-time high in Q1 2026: occupancy climbed to 87.4% with roughly 621,000 occupied units, while construction starts fell to just 1,076 units — the lowest level since 2009 — locking in favorable supply-demand dynamics for operators.

CRE capital markets are thawing as the Fed holds at 3.5–3.75% heading into the April 28–29 FOMC meeting; Colliers forecasts a 15–20% jump in 2026 sales volume, and cap rates are expected to compress 5–15 bps across most property types.

AI has crossed the chasm in real estate: CRE firms running AI pilots jumped from 5% to 92% in three years, with the AI-in-proptech market projected to reach $159.9B by 2033 as agentic AI moves from experimentation to production.

items.headline: Senior housing occupancy hits 87.4% in Q1 2026 as supply stays tight
items.summary: NIC MAP reports Q1 2026 senior living occupancy rose 0.3 points to 87.4% — independent living at 89.0%, assisted living at 85.8% — with a record ~621,000 occupied units. Construction starts fell to 1,076 units, the lowest since Q2 2009, reinforcing strong pricing power for operators like Lloyd Jones through 2027.

items.headline: Fed holds at 3.5–3.75% ahead of April 28–29 FOMC meeting
items.summary: The Federal Reserve left the fed funds target range at 3.5–3.75% for a second straight meeting in March 2026, still signaling one more cut this year. With the 10-Year Treasury stabilized near 4.00–4.25%, CRE borrowers are watching the April 28–29 decision for direction on senior housing refis and acquisition financing.

items.headline: CRE firms running AI pilots surge from 5% to 92% in three years
items.summary: The number of commercial real estate companies running AI pilots jumped from 5% to 92% in just three years, with the AI-in-proptech market projected to reach $159.9B by 2033. Deloitte's 2026 survey names tenant relationship management, lease drafting, and portfolio management as the top deployment areas — all directly relevant to senior housing ops.

items.headline: Colliers forecasts 15–20% jump in 2026 CRE sales volume
items.summary: Institutional and cross-border capital is returning to CRE in 2026, with Colliers projecting a 15–20% rise in sales activity and cap rates compressing 5–15 bps across most property types. Ample dry powder from public and private investors should keep debt markets tight-spread and liquid — a favorable backdrop for senior housing acquisitions.

items.headline: Wellhub: 89% of employees say wellness drives higher performance in 2026
items.summary: Wellhub's 2026 State of Work-Life Wellness survey finds 89% of workers link wellbeing to higher performance, with wellness-focused 'third places' (gyms, yoga studios) displacing bars as the primary venue for workplace relationships. Operators with robust on-site wellness programming — a hallmark of Lloyd Jones' senior living amenity mix — gain recruiting and resident-retention edge.

items.headline: U.S. News names 1,614 communities to 2026 Best Senior Living list
items.summary: U.S. News & World Report released its 2026 Best Senior Living ratings on April 14, recognizing 1,614 communities. The report highlights that the 80+ population will grow nearly 25% in the next five years and double by 2035 — an unprecedented tailwind for senior living supply that remains structurally constrained.

items.headline: Welltower closes $1B Ontario tranche of $4.6B Amica deal
items.summary: Welltower took ownership of nine Amica Senior Lifestyles properties in Ontario as part of its $4.6B Amica acquisition, after agreeing to divest four communities to resolve Canadian competition concerns. The deal is part of Welltower's broader $23B pivot into senior housing — consolidation pressure that reshapes the competitive landscape for mid-market operators.

items.headline: MorningStar + Clarion Partners buy Colorado senior community at 100% occupied
items.summary: MorningStar Senior Living and Clarion Partners acquired MorningStar at Holly Park in Centennial, CO, a community offering IL/AL/memory care that hit 90% occupancy in just nine months and is now fully leased with a waitlist. The deal validates the lease-up speeds achievable in supply-starved metros and signals renewed institutional appetite for Class-A senior housing.

items.headline: CRE loan rates run 4.87%–12.75% as of April 16, 2026
items.summary: Commercial loan rates currently range from 4.87% to 12.75%, with conventional CRE mortgages at 5.07%–8.75% depending on property type and sponsor credit. Most fixed-rate loans remain priced off the 10-Year Treasury, now stable near 4.00–4.25% — creating a narrow but workable underwriting window for senior housing debt.

items.headline: Multifamily rent growth at 0.1% YoY — weakest March since 2012
items.summary: Yardi reported U.S. multifamily advertised rents rose just $5 (0.3%) month-over-month to $1,750 in March, with year-over-year growth stuck at 0.1% — the weakest March since Yardi's records began in 2012. Sun Belt markets continue to lag while Northeast rents grow 4–5%, a divergence that affects underwriting assumptions for multifamily and senior-adjacent assets.

items.headline: Agentic AI becomes operational in real estate workflows
items.summary: 2026 marks the year agentic AI moves from pilot to production in CRE, automating procurement, pricing, inventory, and tenant workflows. Platforms like FlexDesk (used by Reddit and Confluent) are replacing fragmented back-office work with autonomous agents — the template operators should benchmark against for leasing ops and resident services.

items.headline: Deloitte 2026: AI top deployment areas are tenant mgmt, lease drafting, portfolio
items.summary: Deloitte's 2026 commercial real estate outlook survey ranks tenant relationship management, lease drafting, and portfolio management as the top three areas where AI is being implemented today. The shift from isolated tools to measurable operational impact is pressuring vendors to prove ROI — a shift in how owners evaluate proptech stacks.

items.headline: BP names Meg O'Neill CEO effective April 1
items.summary: BP appointed Meg O'Neill, currently CEO of Woodside Energy, as its next chief executive officer effective April 1, 2026. The transition follows Murray Auchincloss's December 2025 departure and signals continued strategic repositioning at the UK oil major — a transition watched closely by energy-sensitive CRE underwriters.

items.headline: Manager engagement dipped from 30% to 27%; 71% of leaders report rising stress
items.summary: Gallup's 2025 data shows a rare drop in manager engagement from 30% to 27%, while 71% of leaders report increased stress and 40% are considering leaving their jobs. 77% of CHROs lack confidence in their bench strength — a warning signal for any operator building multi-site management teams.

items.headline: Hybrid work entrenched: 76% of companies on 3-2 model
items.summary: 76% of companies now operate on a hybrid schedule, most commonly the 3-2 model (3 days in-office, 2 remote), while 26% of remote-capable employees still work fully remote. 62% of remote workers report higher productivity at home, and 71% of managers agree hybrid teams produce more — reshaping office demand and amenity expectations.

items.headline: Conagra taps John Brase as CEO effective June 1
items.summary: Conagra Brands announced April 13 that veteran CPG executive John Brase will become president and CEO on June 1, succeeding Sean Connolly who steps down May 31. The move comes as packaged food companies reset strategy around shifting consumer patterns — relevant for senior housing F&B procurement benchmarks.

items.headline: Armstrong World Industries names Mark Hershey CEO April 1
items.summary: Armstrong World Industries' COO Mark Hershey succeeded Vic Grizzle as president and CEO on April 1, with Grizzle moving to executive chair. Armstrong is a major building products supplier — the leadership change may affect ceiling system pricing and lead times across CRE construction pipelines.

items.headline: Automax.ai uses LiDAR + AI agents to cut appraisals to minutes
items.summary: Proptech startup Automax.ai is using LiDAR and computer vision to auto-capture property details, with custom-trained AI agents generating full appraisal reports in minutes. Faster appraisal turn times could materially compress deal timelines in an environment where Colliers expects 15–20% more transactions in 2026.

items.headline: 'Movement snacks' emerge as top 2026 workplace wellness trend
items.summary: 2026 sees the rise of 'movement snacks' — short bursts of stretching or mobility scattered through the day — as a counter to prolonged sitting. The trend supports joint health and metabolism and is being baked into employer wellness programming and senior living amenity design.

items.headline: Emotional fitness becomes a distinct wellness category in 2026
items.summary: Draxe and other wellness publishers flag 'emotional fitness' as a new 2026 category emphasizing cognitive performance and emotional resilience alongside physical health. Expect it to influence programming at higher-end senior living and wellness-driven multifamily communities — a differentiator in resident experience marketing.
